tasks.title: Intake AI Endpoint + intake_data Consumer
tasks.description: Two connected orphans: (1) intake.html:548 calls POST /ai/intake which does not exist — build the endpoint. It should read intake_data for the family_group_id, pass relevant fields to Claude, and return a structured care plan draft or summary. (2) intake_data is written by intake.html but never consumed anywhere — wire it as input to the AI endpoint and consider surfacing a summary in the care plan editor. Auth: require_practitioner.
tasks.plan:
## Goal
Close two connected orphans in one shot:
- Build `POST /ai/intake` so the intake.html submit flow stops returning 404.
- Wire `intake_data` as both an input (server reads + writes it) and an output (care-plan.html surfaces its content), so it is no longer orphaned.

## Boundary changes
- **New endpoint** `POST /ai/intake` (was ⚠ MISSING). Auth `require_practitioner`. Request `{family_group_id: str, intake: dict}`. Response `{sections: {situation, goals, interventions, family_role, barriers, next_steps}, intake_summary: str}`. Side effect: writes `intake_data/{family_group_id}` server-side via Admin SDK after verifying the caller owns the family_group.
- **Modified collection** `intake_data`: producer moves from `intake.html` (client) to `api/routers/ai.py` (server, Admin SDK). New consumer: `care-plan.html` reads it for the "View intake responses" collapsible.
- **Modified collection** `care_plans`: documents written by the intake flow now carry an `intake_summary` string field alongside `sections`. No rule change (existing practitioner/family/admin rule is fine — family read of intake_summary is intended and not sensitive).
- **No rule changes.** `intake_data` rule stays practitioner-only; Admin SDK bypasses rules for the server write; care-plan.html only reads intake_data when the token has practitioner or admin claim (UI-gated).
- **No new claims, no new indexes** (intake_data is doc-id-keyed).

## Implementation steps

### 1. Registry updates first (docs/registries/)
- `endpoints.md`: replace the `POST /ai/intake ⚠ MISSING` block with a served entry — served by `api/routers/ai.py`, callers `intake.html:548`, request/response shape above, status ✓. Update the summary table row.
- `collections.md` `intake_data` section: remove the ⚠ Orphaned line; producers become `api/routers/ai.py` (server, Admin SDK after ownership check); consumers become `api/routers/ai.py` (server, for downstream Claude call) and `care-plan.html` (UI collapsible, practitioner/admin only).
- `collections.md` `care_plans` schema line: add `intake_summary?: string` to the key fields.

### 2. Backend — `api/services/claude.py`
Add `draft_intake_care_plan(intake: dict) -> dict`:
- Build a prompt that flattens the intake dict into a readable section-by-section block (client_context, current_situation, medical_history, care_needs, goals).
- Instruct Claude to return ONLY a JSON object with these exact keys: `situation`, `goals`, `interventions`, `family_role`, `barriers`, `next_steps`, `intake_summary`. Each section is a string (markdown allowed). `intake_summary` is one short paragraph (3–5 sentences) synthesizing the case.
- Call `complete(prompt, model="claude-sonnet-4-6", max_tokens=2048)`.
- Parse JSON. Strip ```json fences if present. On parse failure, return `{"sections": {}, "intake_summary": <raw_text>}` so the client still has something usable.
- Do not change `draft_care_plan` (orphan, different signature, leave alone).

### 3. Backend — `api/routers/ai.py`
Add Pydantic model `IntakeRequest(BaseModel)` with `family_group_id: str` and `intake: dict` (loose typing — the intake structure is owned by the client form).

Add handler `@router.post("/intake")`:
- `user=Depends(require_practitioner)`
- `db = get_db()`; load `family_groups/{family_group_id}`; 404 if missing; 403 if `practitioner_id != user["uid"]`.
- Write `intake_data/{family_group_id}` with `set(..., merge=True)`: `{family_group_id, practitioner_id: user["uid"], intake, updated_at: serverTimestamp(), created_at: serverTimestamp() if doc didn't exist}`. Use `firestore.SERVER_TIMESTAMP`.
- Call `draft_intake_care_plan(body.intake)`; pull `intake_summary` and the six section keys out of the result.
- Return `{"sections": {situation, goals, interventions, family_role, barriers, next_steps}, "intake_summary": ...}`.

### 4. Frontend — `intake.html`
- Remove the client-side `setDoc(doc(db, 'intake_data', familyId), ...)` write at line ~575 (success path). Server now owns this write.
- Remove the duplicate `setDoc('intake_data', ...)` write in the catch-block fallback at line ~591. The server may or may not have written it depending on where the failure occurred; client should not re-write.
- Keep the `setDoc('care_plans', familyId, ...)` write; add `intake_summary: result.intake_summary || ''` to the payload alongside `sections`.
- No change to the request body shape (`{family_group_id, intake}`).

### 5. Frontend — `care-plan.html`
- In `renderPlan()`, if `planData.intake_summary` is non-empty, render a labeled card above the section list: header "From Intake — Summary" + the summary text. Read-only.
- Add an "Intake Responses" collapsible (closed by default), shown only when the current token claims include `practitioner` or `admin` (not `family`). On first expand, `getDoc(doc(db, 'intake_data', familyId))`; render each top-level intake field (`client_context`, `current_situation`, `medical_history`, `care_needs`, `goals`) as a sub-block with its labeled sub-fields. If the doc doesn't exist, show "No intake on file."
- Plain HTML/JS, no new libraries. Reuse existing `.plan-section` styling for the summary card; the collapsible can be a `<details>` element to avoid custom toggle code.

### 6. Verification (manual — no test suite in repo)
1. Run the API locally with `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` set; open `/docs` (dev only).
2. Sign in as a practitioner with a test family group. Navigate to `intake.html?id=<groupId>`, fill all 5 steps with synthetic content, submit.
3. Confirm: network tab shows `POST /ai/intake` 200; response has 6 section keys + `intake_summary`; the success view renders.
4. Open `care-plan.html?id=<groupId>` as the same practitioner. Confirm: the "From Intake — Summary" card appears with the synthesis; "Intake Responses" collapsible shows the raw nested intake fields when expanded.
5. Confirm Firestore: `intake_data/{groupId}` exists with the submitted form; `care_plans/{groupId}` has `sections.*` populated and `intake_summary` set, `ai_generated: true`.
6. Edge: submit as a practitioner who does NOT own the group → endpoint returns 403. Submit with an unknown `family_group_id` → 404.
7. Edge: as a family member, open `care-plan.html` for a group they belong to → the summary card renders (family read of care_plans is allowed) but the "Intake Responses" collapsible is hidden.

### 7. Bump + deploy
- Bump `api/main.py` `version="0.1.1"` → `0.1.2` in the same commit.
- Push to main per project workflow; existing CI deploys the API.

## Out of scope
- No refactor of `draft_care_plan` / `POST /ai/care-plan-draft` (still orphan; addressed by a future task if ever).
- No streaming responses; the existing fixed-interval status text in intake.html stays.
- No edit/regenerate UI for the AI output beyond the existing care-plan editor.

tasks.title: Manage Family Members UI
tasks.description: The POST /family-groups/{id}/revoke endpoint exists and is tested but has no UI. Build a 'Manage Family Members' section in client.html (or a dedicated page) that lists current family members for a group (name, email, invited_at, status) and allows the practitioner to revoke access with a confirmation step. Read family member list from family_invitations collection filtered by group_id and status=accepted. After revoke, update the list in real time.
tasks.plan:
## Boundary changes

- **Collections:** none new. `family_invitations` gains a new consumer (client.html, practitioner-side read of accepted docs for a group). No new write path.
- **Endpoints:** no new endpoints. Wires up the existing orphan `POST /family-groups/{group_id}/revoke` as its first caller.
- **Claims:** none.
- **Rules:** none. Existing `/family_invitations` rule already allows the owning practitioner to read (firestore.rules:151-154). Practitioner reading family-role `users/{accepted_by_uid}` docs is NOT allowed by current rules, so the UI displays `invited_email` as the identity — no name lookup. (Denormalising `display_name` onto the invitation at accept time is a separate follow-up if email-only proves too thin.)
- **Indexes:** existing `(family_group_id ASC, invited_at DESC)` on family_invitations (firestore.indexes.json:43-50) already backs the query — no new index needed.
- **UI:** new tab on client.html (no new page). Practitioner-only — gated by existing client.html auth (client.html:681).

## Steps

**1. Registry updates (docs first)**
- `docs/registries/collections.md` — `family_invitations` section: add client.html (practitioner-side) as a consumer (read accepted list for a group via the existing index).
- `docs/registries/endpoints.md` — `POST /family-groups/{group_id}/revoke`: change Callers from "none currently" to `client.html` (manage-family-members panel); drop the orphan ⚠ flag in body and in the summary table.
- `docs/contracts/family-member-flow.md` — UI promises section: replace "Family settings / manage family members — out of scope, deferred to a later contract" with a one-line note that the practitioner-side panel lives on client.html and wires the existing revoke endpoint.

**2. Firestore rules** — no changes. Re-verify firestore.rules:146-163: owning practitioner can read `family_invitations` where `family_group_id` matches.

**3. Firestore indexes** — no changes. The `(family_group_id ASC, invited_at DESC)` composite at firestore.indexes.json:43-50 covers `where(family_group_id==X).orderBy(invited_at desc)`.

**4. Backend** — no code changes. The existing `revoke_family_member` at api/routers/family_groups.py:226 already removes the group from `family_group_ids`, revokes refresh tokens, updates the user doc, and marks any matching `family_invitations` row as `status='revoked'` with `revoked_at`. That last write is the signal the UI's onSnapshot listener uses to drop the member in real time.

**5. Frontend — client.html**
  - Add a new tab button after the existing tabs (client.html:485-489): `<button class="tab" data-tab="family">Family Members</button>`.
  - Add a new `<div class="tab-panel" id="panel-family">` after the Crisis Card panel (after client.html:635). Contains a section-card with header "Family Members" and a body div `id="familyMembersList"` plus an empty state.
  - Add an `API_BASE` constant near the top of the script section (mirroring new-client.html:640): `const API_BASE = 'https://careguide-api-658340465706.us-central1.run.app';`. (client.html currently has none.)
  - Add `subscribeFamilyMembers(familyId)` — uses Firestore `onSnapshot` on `query(collection(db,'family_invitations'), where('family_group_id','==',familyId), orderBy('invited_at','desc'))`. Renders only docs where `status === 'accepted'`. Each row shows: `invited_email`, `invited_relationship` (when present), formatted `accepted_at` (fallback `invited_at`), and a `Revoke access` button. Button is disabled with tooltip if `accepted_by_uid` is missing (defensive — older docs pre-uid-capture).
  - Add `revokeFamilyMember(uid, email)` — `confirm(\`Revoke access for ${email}? They will lose access immediately.\`)`; POST to `${API_BASE}/family-groups/${familyId}/revoke` with `{uid}` body and `Authorization: Bearer ${idToken}`. On non-2xx, `showToast(...)` a friendly error. On success, do nothing — onSnapshot picks up the status flip.
  - Wire `subscribeFamilyMembers(familyId)` into the page load flow alongside the other loaders; keep the unsubscribe handle for navigation cleanup.
  - Empty state copy: "No family members have accepted invitations yet."

**6. Wiring check**
  - Producer: `revoke_family_member` writes `status='revoked'` on the invitation (api/routers/family_groups.py:283).
  - Consumer: new onSnapshot listener filters to `status==='accepted'`, so the revoked member disappears within ~1s.
  - Practitioner-only: client.html gates to practitioner|admin (client.html:681), and `/family_invitations` rule requires `ownsGroup`, so a non-owner practitioner cannot read the list.

**7. Verification (manual, end-to-end)**
  1. Sign in as a practitioner who owns a family group with at least one accepted family member (use existing data or run the invite + accept flow).
  2. Open `/client.html?id={groupId}`; click the new **Family Members** tab.
  3. Confirm the list shows the family member's email and accepted date.
  4. Click **Revoke access**, confirm the prompt.
  5. Row disappears within ~1s.
  6. In a separate browser signed in as that family member, next request to `/family.html` fails / forces re-auth (refresh tokens were revoked) and the group is no longer accessible.
  7. Multi-group case: revoke when the user has multiple group memberships — confirm only the one group is removed (verify via response `remaining_groups` or Firebase console).
  8. Idempotent case: revoke a uid already missing the group — endpoint returns `revoked: false`; UI should not throw.
  9. Legacy case: an `accepted` invitation missing `accepted_by_uid` — Revoke button stays disabled with tooltip; console log surfaces the doc id.

## Dependencies
None — task is independent.

## Files touched (expected)
- `client.html` (new tab + panel + ~60 lines of JS + API_BASE constant)
- `docs/registries/collections.md`
- `docs/registries/endpoints.md`
- `docs/contracts/family-member-flow.md`

No backend, rules, or index changes.

tasks.title: Practitioner Messaging UI
tasks.description: family_messages collection has rules for practitioner read/write but client.html has no messaging UI. Build a messaging thread in client.html that shows the full family_messages thread for a group (ordered by created_at) and allows the practitioner to post replies. Family members already send messages from family.html. This closes the two-way messaging loop. Real-time via onSnapshot.

tasks.title: Profession + Beta Claims Audit
tasks.description: profession and beta claims are set during registration and approval but checked nowhere. Audit and decide: (a) wire profession claim into dashboard filtering or UI labeling (e.g. show discipline badge on practitioner profile), (b) wire beta claim into feature flagging for any beta-only features, or (c) if neither has a real use case yet, remove the claim setters to reduce orphan surface. Document the decision in claims registry regardless.

tasks.title: Orphan API Router Cleanup
tasks.description: parents, medications, and session-notes routers exist in the API but all client pages use Firestore directly instead of calling them. Decide and execute: either (a) remove the routers (simpler, reduces surface) or (b) migrate the client pages to use the API routers (adds auth enforcement at API layer, enables server-side validation). Document the decision. If removing, update endpoints registry. If keeping, wire the clients and add integration tests.

tasks.title: Supabase Project Setup + Schema
tasks.description: Initialize the Postgres/Supabase reporting layer. Tasks: (1) Create Supabase project and store credentials in env/secrets, (2) Design the reporting schema — tables for practitioners, family_groups, parents, medications, session_notes, care_updates, family_members, care_plans — with proper foreign keys and timestamps, (3) Write and run the initial migration, (4) Add Supabase Python client to api/requirements.txt, (5) Create a supabase.py service in api/services/. No data sync yet — just the foundation.

tasks.title: Firestore → Supabase Sync Layer
tasks.description: Mirror Firestore writes into Supabase for reporting. For each write endpoint in the API (register, accept-invite, family group creation, care plan updates, medications, session notes, care updates), add a fire-and-forget Supabase upsert after the Firestore write succeeds. Do not block the response on the Supabase write — log failures. Initially sync: users, family_groups, parents, medications, session_notes, care_updates, care_plans. Add a /admin/sync-status endpoint that shows last sync timestamps per collection.

tasks.title: Practitioner Analytics Dashboard
tasks.description: Build a practitioner-facing analytics section showing: active client count, session note frequency (notes per week/month), care plan completion rate, family engagement (care updates posted, family logins), medication change history. Data sourced from Supabase. New API endpoints: GET /analytics/practitioner (requires practitioner claim). New UI section in dashboard.html or a dedicated analytics.html page. Charts via a lightweight charting library (e.g. Chart.js via CDN).

tasks.title: Admin Platform Analytics
tasks.description: Build admin-facing platform analytics: total practitioners (active/pending/rejected), total family groups, total family members, care updates posted per week, new signups over time, practitioner activity (last active). Data sourced from Supabase. New API endpoints: GET /analytics/platform (requires admin claim). New section in admin-approvals.html or dedicated admin-analytics.html. Timeframe filter (7d, 30d, 90d, all-time).
